LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT GREENE MOTORS, INC. DBA AVERY GREENE HONDA'S PETITION TO COMPEL BINDING ARBITRATION AND TO STAY THE INSTANT ACTION IS OFF CALENDAR FROM JAN-31-2014 DUPLICATE ENTRY.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT GREENE MOTORS, INC. DBA AVERY GREENE HONDA'S PETITION TO COMPEL BINDING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE ARBITRATION BEFORE THE AMERICAN ARBITRATION ASSOCIATION OR MUTUALLY AGREED UPON ALTERNATIVE FORUM. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HER BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE ARBITRATION PROVISION IS UNCONSCIONABLE. PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE WOULD GIVE HER UNILATERAL AUTHORITY TO CHOOSE THE ARBITRATION FORUM, AND THE ARBITRATION PROVISION IS NOT VOID AS ILLUSORY. ALTHOUGH THERE IS MINIMAL EVIDENCE OF PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THE HIGH DEGREE OF SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY NECESSARY TO RENDER THE ARBITRATION PROVISION UNENFORCEABLE. THE ARBITRATION PROVISION PROVIDES THAT GREENE MOTORS, INC. SHALL PAY THE FIRST $ 5000 OF PLAINTIFF'S ARBITRATION COSTS. ONE ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR# 12353.

Joinder In Petition To Compel Binding Arb And Stay Action
Off Calendar Mar-14-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Francis D. Conway, Esq. of Parton Sell Rhoades, Tel. 415.258.9700, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'s Joinder In Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration And Stay Action is granted. The action is stayed pending the arbitration before the American Arbitration Association or mutually agreed upon alternative forum. Plaintiff has not met her burden to demonstrate that the arbitration provision is unconscionable. Plaintiff's interpretation of the arbitration clause would give her unilateral authority to choose the arbitration forum, and the arbitration provision is not void as illusory. Although there is minimal evidence of procedural unconscionability, Plaintiff has not demonstrated the high degree of substantive unconscionability necessary to render the arbitration provision unenforceable. The arbitration provision provides that Greene Motors, Inc. shall pay the first $ 5000 of Plaintiff's arbitration costs. One order signed for both motions. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR# 12353.

Notice Of Motion And M9otion To Dismiss Of Transfer Action For Improper Venue; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: KRISTIN KEMNITZER, ESQ., 415.632.1900, FOR PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA NICHOLAS.
THOMAS CROWELL, ESQ., 510.835.3400, FOR EFENDANT GREENE MOTORS, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant GREENE MOTORS, INC. dba AVERY GREENE HONDA'S Motion To Dismiss Of Transfer Action For Improper Venue; Request For Sanctions IS Denied. A defendant is a sham defendant if it appears from the complaint that a cause of action cannot be stated against that defendant. (Konnoff v. Fraser (1944) 62 Cal.App.2d 788, 791-92.) Here, the complaint pleads a cause of action under the Consumers Legal Remedy Act against Defendant Honda. It is true that the main allegations are principally based on allegations of misrepresentations by Greene Motors. The complaint, however, liberally construed as the Court is required to do, also alleges that it was represented to Plaintiff that she was purchasing a Honda Certified Used Car. (Complaint, 12.) This arguably passed on to Plaintiff a representation by Honda itself that, by certifying the used car, Honda represented that the car had been thoroughly inspected and met certain standards. (Id.) The complaint alleges that Civ. Code 1770(a)(5) was violated because the car is unsafe to operate on a roadway, which directly contradicts the representations made by Honda through its certification. (Id., 22.) So construed, the facts pled sufficiently state a cause of action against Honda. Therefore, Honda is not a sham defendant and may be considered for venue purposes. Under Civ. Code, 1780(d)), venue is proper in the county in which the defendant "is doing business." Defendant Greene Motors, Inc. has not met its burden in establishing that Honda does not do business in San Francisco. Because venue is proper in San Francisco for Defendant Honda, venue is proper in San Francisco for all defendants. (Monogram Co. of Cal. v. Kingsley (1951) 38 Cal.2d 28, 29-30.). Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
